sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: KVC Westerlo have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins and 3 losses, scoring 11 goals but also conceding 11. Standard Liège are struggling, with no wins in their last 4 matches, managing only 6 goals while conceding 10 in their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 encounters between these teams, matches have been tight, resulting in a 0-0 draw and a 1-1 draw. Westerlo have not lost to Standard Liège in their last 9 head-to-head games, indicating a slight historical edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Westerlo rank higher in the league (11th) compared to Standard Liège (14th), with a better goal-scoring record (11 vs 6). Both teams struggle defensively, conceding a similar number of goals, but Westerlo's attacking output and home advantage could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends
sections.text: Westerlo matches often see over 2.5 goals (7/9 games), and they average high corner counts at home. Standard Liège have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 6 matches, which could play into Westerlo's favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Standard Liège leads KVC Westerlo with a comfortable 2-0 scoreline. The away side capitalized on their opportunities with two well-taken goals in the first half, showcasing their clinical finishing. Westerlo, despite having equal ball possession, struggled to convert their presence in the final third into meaningful chances. The home team will need to regroup and find a way to break through Standard Liège's defense if they hope to turn this game around in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: KVC Westerlo showed determination in the first half, managing a decent share of possession and making numerous entries into the final third. However, their inability to create big chances and lack of shots on target highlight a lack of cutting edge in attack. Defensively, they’ve been vulnerable, allowing Standard Liège to capitalize on key moments. On the other hand, Standard Liège has been efficient, making the most of their limited penalty area entries to score twice. Their defensive solidity, despite less possession in certain phases, has kept Westerlo at bay.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Standard Liège, Casper Nielsen and Adnane Abid have been pivotal, with both finding the back of the net in the first half. Abid also contributed with an assist, underlining his importance to the team’s attacking output. Their midfield control and finishing have given their side a strong foothold in this match. For Westerlo, Griffin Yow has been a bright spot with a shot on target and decent involvement in the attack, but overall, the team lacks a clinical finisher to convert their efforts into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Early on, Standard Liège dominated the attacking play with consistent pressure in the opening minutes. However, Westerlo responded with a strong spell midway through the half, pushing forward with intent and maintaining prolonged periods of attack. Despite this, Standard Liège regained momentum around key moments, especially leading up to their goals. Towards the end of the half, Westerlo once again pressed hard, but their efforts lacked the final product to trouble the scoreboard.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, KVC Westerlo will likely come out with renewed energy, desperate to claw their way back into the game. They may focus on utilizing their aerial strength and creating more direct chances in the penalty area. Standard Liège, however, will aim to protect their lead, potentially sitting back and looking for counter-attacking opportunities. If Westerlo can find an early breakthrough, it could shift the game’s dynamics, but Standard Liège’s defensive organization might prove difficult to penetrate.
